Heap-use-after-free READ (1 byte) in cff_blend_doBlend() (src/cff/cffload.c), reached via cff_parser_run -> cff_load_private_dict while parsing a CFF Private DICT with two or more consecutive `blend` operators. Each blend op may need to grow subFont->blend_stack via FT_REALLOC; when realloc moves the buffer, blend_top is correctly reset, but parser->stack entries from a *previous* blend op in the same DICT still point into the old (now-freed) allocation. A second blend op in a row reads through one of those stale parser->stack pointers, hitting freed heap memory. Fix adjusts every parser->stack pointer that fell inside the old buffer by the realloc's address delta.

 #1 0x5dea62 in match /src/pcre2/src/pcre2_match.c:819:5
 #2 0x59ae16 in pcre2_match_8 /src/pcre2/src/pcre2_match.c:6583:8
 #3 0x56c714 in LLVMFuzzerTestOneInput /src/pcre2/src/pcre2_fuzzsupport.c:193:19
DEDUP_TOKEN: __asan_memcpy--match--pcre2_match_8
Address 0x71510b9ce170 is located in stack of thread T0 at offset 10608 in frame
This frame has 2 object(s):
 [32, 296) 'actual_match_block' (line 6022)
 [368, 10608) 'stack_frames_vector' (line 6030) <== Memory access at offset 10608 overflows this variable
SUMMARY: AddressSanitizer: stack-buffer-overflow /src/llvm-project/compiler-rt/lib/asan/asan_interceptors_memintrinsics.cpp:22:3 in __asan_memcpy

Heap-buffer-overflow READ in pcre2_fuzzsupport.c. The fuzz harness pre-scans the pattern for quantifier sequences like ')digits{' so it can clamp oversized quantifiers before calling pcre2_compile(). The scan loop `for (size_t i = 1; i < size - 2; i++)` computes `size - 2` on an unsigned size_t; for inputs shorter than 3 bytes this underflows to a near-SIZE_MAX value instead of the loop being skipped, so the scan reads far past the end of the heap-allocated input buffer.

Wild/NULL-based pointer read in xmlDictFindEntry() (dict.c), reached via xmlDictLookupInternal() when a dict has a subdict that is non-NULL but empty (subdict->size == 0, i.e. subdict->table was never grown and is NULL). The pre-patch code only checks `dict->subdict != NULL` before searching it. Inside xmlDictFindEntry, `mask = dict->size - 1` is computed on an unsigned field, so size==0 underflows to UINT_MAX; `pos = hashValue & mask` is then effectively unmasked, and `entry = &dict->table[pos]` produces a wild pointer offset from a NULL table. Dereferencing `entry->hashValue` crashes. Note: the OSS-Fuzz report was actually filed against the libxslt 'xpath' fuzzer (afl_asan_libxslt), not a libxml2-specific fuzzer -- it reaches xmlDictLookupInternal via xmlNewDocNode, but the fix landed in libxml2's dict.c since that's where the bug lives.
